raw_text:

{
  "claims": [
    "AB 427 would allow home and car battery owners to feed excess power into the state’s grid during peak usage.",
    "Feeding excess power into the grid could prevent blackouts and reduce fossil fuel emissions.",
    "California's power grid was significantly underestimated during a 2020 heat wave, resulting in over 800,000 power outages.",
    "Excess clean energy stored in solar-powered home batteries and electric cars could mitigate power supply gaps during high-demand periods.",
    "Regulatory barriers prevent the aggregation and sale of excess clean energy to the grid.",
    "AB 427 requires the California Public Utilities Commission to develop guidelines for purchasing excess energy from consumers.",

    "Data collected from indirect pollution sources will enable evaluation of health impacts on surrounding communities.",
    "With better data on pollution sources, air districts can seek innovative ways to reduce toxic air contaminants.",
    "Transportation and goods movement emissions can significantly impact local public health, particularly near large facilities."
